title: Arrhenatherum compactum
taxonomy: Arrhenatherum compactum is classified as a perennial grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The genus Arrhenatherum is a monotypic genus, consisting of only one species. The species Arrhenatherum compactum is native to the Mediterranean region and Europe. It is also known as compact oat-grass due to its compact growth habit.
uses: Arrhenatherum compactum, also known as compact oat grass, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis and gout. The plant is also said to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, potentially alleviating symptoms of indigestion and other gastrointestinal issues. In addition, Arrhenatherum compactum has been used to treat respiratory problems, including bronchitis and coughs, due to its supposed expectorant properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system.
